item.title: OpenAI to double workforce as business push intensifies
item.description: The $730bn start-up plans to increase staff to 8,000 by end of 2026 in bid to close gap with rival Anthropic

item.title: How the FTSE got comfortable with bumper pay for bosses
item.description: Generous deals to keep CEOs at some of the largest UK companies have caused barely a ripple of discontent

item.title: Canada’s oil producers in line for C$90bn windfall from Iran war
item.description: Rising crude price will boost export revenue as Mark Carney seeks to counter Donald Trump’s trade wars

item.title: US fertiliser bosses cash in as Iran war boosts shares
item.description: CF Industries aided by access to low-cost US natural gas as energy crisis slams Asian and European competitors

item.title: Sir James Dyson tackles rugby
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: the WNBA’s new labour deal and Fifa’s World Cup reliance

item.title: Iran lets grain ships through Hormuz to shore up food supply
item.description: Agriculture has been a ‘pain point’ for Tehran after it imposes de facto blockade of waterway in response to US-Israeli war

item.title: Uber fights to stay in the robotaxi race
item.description: Ride-hailing company must make up lost ground on driverless vehicles, or risk being left in the rear-view mirror

item.title: Life insurance sales surge in wake of Reeves’ inheritance tax shake-up
item.description: Chancellor’s targeting of pensions prompts more people to take out insurance, say industry figures

item.title: EU member states urged to lower gas storage targets due to Iran war
item.description: European Commission proposes steps to try to reduce demand for energy from households and industry

item.title: We should all be playing pothole politics
item.description: Living in the ‘Potswolds’ has made me one of the Angry Local People pointing at our damaged roads

item.title: Aston Martin could do with a carpool companion
item.description: Brand still has global cachet but it is finding life tricky as a tiny luxury-car maker

item.title: Britain must be more vigilant to the risk of sabotage by hostile states
item.description: If Moscow and Beijing are determined to destabilise the west then new strategies are needed

item.title: Cargill uses AI to get more meat from the bone as beef prices soar
item.description: System called CarVe spots flecks of meat on carcasses as they move along the production line

item.title: P. Louise founder: ‘When you come from the bottom you have nothing to lose’
item.description: Self-taught make-up artist Paige Louise Williams has turned her beauty business into one of the UK’s fastest growing

item.title: Japan’s bullet trains shift to cargo as seats go unfilled
item.description: High-speed rail freight services aim to offset truck driver shortages and dwindling passenger numbers

item.title: Taiwan concerned by depletion of US missile stocks during Iran war
item.description: Some weapons used in Gulf would be crucial in early phases of any conflict with China

item.title: Amazon-backed nuclear reactor group X-energy files for IPO
item.description: Start-up aims to capitalise on rising interest in atomic power due to growing power demand from AI

item.title: Musk defrauded Twitter investors during buyout, jury finds
item.description: Panel members conclude the billionaire misled shareholders in tweets posted during the $44bn takeover

item.title: Can Europe break free from US tech dominance? Submit your questions
item.description: Take part in a live Q&A with FT Innovation Editor John Thornhill and EU correspondent Barbara Moens, on Thursday March 26 at 1pm GMT

item.title: Big drugmakers saved at least $5bn on US taxes shifting income overseas
item.description: New disclosures show how pharma groups reduced federal levies last year by assigning profits to low-tax jurisdictions

item.title: Flagship Blackstone credit fund posts first monthly loss since 2022
item.description: Loan markdowns and market declines led to negative return for the first time in more than three years

item.title: Supermicro drops 33% after co-founder charged with smuggling Nvidia chips to China
item.description: US prosecutors allege two employees and a contractor conspired to illegally ship servers through south-east Asia

item.title: Goldman lawyer who resigned over Epstein links paid $25mn in 2025
item.description: Kathy Ruemmler to step down as the bank’s general counsel at the end of June

item.title: US to deploy thousands more troops to the Middle East
item.description: Marines and sailors to arrive in the region in three to four weeks, signalling possible new phase in conflict

item.title: Airline industry hit by biggest crisis since pandemic
item.description: Middle East war has unleashed severe disruption for the aviation sector

item.title: BA owner IAG threatens to walk away from bid for TAP
item.description: Group has warned Portuguese government it will only bid for airline if majority ownership rule is relaxed

item.title: A bet on Bill Ackman’s new listing is complex
item.description: The hedge fund manager is seeking to extend his Pershing Square portfolio in multiple vehicles

item.title: Stockpickers: Essentra, Wickes, Trustpilot
item.description: Our experts discuss which companies to buy, sell or hold this week

item.title: Directors’ Deals: IAG insiders divest ahead of war-induced sell-off
item.description: Airline group’s shares have fallen by a quarter since the attack on Iran

item.title: Iran war will scar the global economy
item.description: Scenarios that markets and policymakers hoped to avoid are now materialising
